rationale: In the citing paper, Kirmani and Zhu (2007) appears only in the General Discussion as part of a grouped citation supporting a broad claim that consumers may recognize marketers' persuasive motives. The paper does not use this source to derive core hypotheses, operationalize constructs, design experiments, or provide a paper-specific mechanism test tied to that prior work. Its role is background positioning within persuasion-knowledge literature rather than a distinct building block for the comfort-tier framework.
